covered_report_window: Monday, June 15, 2026 after U.S. market close through Tuesday, June 16, 2026 before U.S. market open.
market_context_summary: U.S. market was still open at run time. Risk appetite was constructive with tech/AI leadership, oil easing on U.S.-Iran peace headlines, and the week shortened by the Friday Juneteenth market holiday. The earnings window is small-cap and mid-cap heavy, so liquidity, filing headlines, options volatility, and management tone are unusually important.

ranked_companies.timing: June 16, 2026 before market open; call 10:00 a.m. ET
ranked_companies.one_sentence_thesis: Wiley has the cleanest positive setup because Research, AI/data services, margin expansion, cash flow, and buybacks give investors multiple verifiable ways to reward the print if Q4 confirms guidance quality.
ranked_companies.market_expectation_summary: Kiplinger lists a Tuesday before-open report with EPS estimate of $1.65; Wiley's Q3 release guided FY2026 adjusted EBITDA margin and adjusted EPS to the high end of prior ranges while reaffirming revenue and free cash flow.
ranked_companies.upside_case_summary: Research Publishing and AI/data services momentum continue, adjusted EBITDA margin stays near the high end, and free cash flow supports buybacks/dividend.
ranked_companies.downside_case_summary: Learning/professional softness, a merely in-line FY2027 outlook, or AI revenue being too small to change growth perception could cap reaction.
ranked_companies.options_or_positioning_summary: Options data is less central; MarketChameleon snippets show prior WLY earnings moves can be double-digit, but evidence quality is stronger on fundamentals than positioning.
ranked_companies.analyst_revision_summary: Consensus sources show positive EPS expectations; Q3 management raised the effective earnings/margin bar by guiding to high end of range.

ranked_companies.timing: June 15, 2026 after market close; call 5:00 p.m. ET
ranked_companies.one_sentence_thesis: Quantum leans up because preliminary Q4 revenue was materially above guidance, but the positive surprise is partly pre-announced and offset by late-filing and balance-sheet risk.
ranked_companies.market_expectation_summary: The company preannounced Q4 revenue of about $77.5 million plus or minus $2 million versus prior guidance of $68 million plus or minus $2 million, and also announced a same-day NT 10-K filing and recent equity financing.
ranked_companies.upside_case_summary: Full results confirm the revenue beat, gross margin/EBITDA hold, AI-ready infrastructure backlog improves, and debt/liquidity commentary is reassuring.
ranked_companies.downside_case_summary: Late filing, cash balance, dilution, or weak FY2027 outlook could overwhelm the revenue beat.
ranked_companies.options_or_positioning_summary: TipRanks listed an implied move around 19.58% for QMCO, signaling high event volatility; recent preannouncement reduces surprise quality.
ranked_companies.analyst_revision_summary: Sparse coverage; published debate centers on whether revenue strength converts into durable profitability and balance-sheet improvement.

ranked_companies.timing: Likely June 15, 2026 after market close release; webcast June 16 at 11:30 a.m. ET
ranked_companies.expected_driver: Retail gross margin and EBITDA conversion
ranked_companies.one_sentence_thesis: High Tide leans up if Q2 confirms record-revenue momentum and brick-and-mortar margin progress, but cannabis-sector volatility and a low-priced stock keep reversal risk elevated.
ranked_companies.market_expectation_summary: High Tide scheduled a June 16 webcast after the Q2 release; FMP/Zacks-type sources show a June 15 release expectation and EPS around a small loss, while Q1 revenue grew 25% year over year to CAD 178.3 million.
ranked_companies.upside_case_summary: Canna Cabana margin expansion, Elite loyalty growth, positive free cash flow, and adjusted EBITDA durability continue.
ranked_companies.downside_case_summary: Revenue beat without margin progress, cash-flow slippage, regulatory/cannabis sentiment, or profit-taking after a good story could reverse the move.
ranked_companies.options_or_positioning_summary: TipRanks listed HITI implied move around 13.42%; MarketChameleon snippets show prior earnings moved materially but within high implied volatility.
ranked_companies.analyst_revision_summary: Public estimates point to a small loss; revisions data is limited, so operating KPIs matter more than EPS.

ranked_companies.one_sentence_thesis: Vince has a modest upside setup if Q1 shows DTC-led growth and tariff mitigation, but Saks exposure, tariff assumptions, and small-cap liquidity keep it out of the priority basket.
ranked_companies.market_expectation_summary: Vince officially scheduled a June 16 pre-market Q1 release. Q4 net sales grew 4.7%, DTC rose 10.4%, and FY2026 guidance called for 3%-6% sales growth with adjusted EBITDA margin around 5%-5.5% assuming a 15% tariff rate.
ranked_companies.upside_case_summary: DTC growth remains strong, gross margin holds despite tariffs, and Saks-related credit noise does not worsen.
ranked_companies.downside_case_summary: Tariff pressure, wholesale softness, Saks-related receivables, or weak operating margin guidance could dominate.
ranked_companies.options_or_positioning_summary: No meaningful implied-move data found; low liquidity and small float matter more than listed options.
ranked_companies.analyst_revision_summary: Zacks/MarketBeat-type sources imply a Q1 loss; revisions are sparse and evidence quality is limited.

ranked_companies.timing: June 15, 2026 after market close; call 5:00 p.m. ET
ranked_companies.one_sentence_thesis: Dave & Buster's has a plausible low-bar relief setup, but the market needs proof that comparable sales and traffic have stabilized enough to offset weak FY2025 profitability and a volatile options setup.
ranked_companies.market_expectation_summary: Kiplinger lists expected EPS of $0.60. The prior Q4 showed revenue down 0.9%, comparable sales down 3.3%, adjusted EBITDA down year over year, and FY2025 comparable sales down 5.0%.
ranked_companies.upside_case_summary: Comps stabilize or inflect, management gives credible traffic/game-room improvement, and low expectations create relief.
ranked_companies.downside_case_summary: Another comp decline, margin pressure, weak consumer spend, or a vague turnaround update could sink the stock even if EPS clears consensus.
ranked_companies.options_or_positioning_summary: TipRanks listed a large implied move around 17.79%, and the stock had rebounded from deep 52-week weakness into the event, raising both squeeze and sell-the-news risk.
ranked_companies.analyst_revision_summary: Consensus EPS is positive, but the key metric is same-store sales and management's forward commentary rather than headline EPS alone.

ranked_companies.timing: June 16, 2026 before U.S. market open; call 8:00 a.m. ET
ranked_companies.one_sentence_thesis: Uxin's unit and revenue growth are real, but thin ADS trading, losses, low gross margin, and guidance that already embeds strong growth leave no clean directional edge.
ranked_companies.market_expectation_summary: The company scheduled June 16 before-open results; Q4 2025 retail transaction volume rose 124% year over year, total revenue rose 100.7%, but gross margin was only 6.8% and adjusted EBITDA remained negative.
ranked_companies.upside_case_summary: Retail units and revenue meet or beat guidance, new superstores ramp smoothly, and losses narrow.
ranked_companies.downside_case_summary: Gross margin compression, higher selling expenses, weak cash generation, or China ADR sentiment could offset growth.
ranked_companies.options_or_positioning_summary: No reliable implied-move data found; liquidity and ADR risk are the main positioning concerns.
ranked_companies.analyst_revision_summary: Traditional U.S. consensus coverage is limited; company guidance for Q1 retail volume and revenue is more useful than EPS consensus.

ranked_companies.timing: June 15, 2026 after market close; call 5:00 p.m. ET
ranked_companies.expected_driver: Filing delay and consumption ARR quality
ranked_companies.one_sentence_thesis: Domo is the cleanest downside setup because the earnings delay and NT 10-Q plan raise process risk, even though consumption ARR and retention metrics can still produce a relief rally if the numbers are clean.
ranked_companies.market_expectation_summary: Domo rescheduled Q1 FY2027 results from June 9 to June 15 because it needed more time to complete financial statements and planned to file an NT 10-Q; Q4 had strong consumption-cohort NRR and operating margin records.
ranked_companies.upside_case_summary: Results are filed cleanly, consumption ARR/NRR remains strong, AI analytics messaging improves, and guidance clears a low bar.
ranked_companies.downside_case_summary: Delay-related accounting/process concerns, weak current RPO, soft guidance, or slowing subscription growth could dominate.
ranked_companies.options_or_positioning_summary: TipRanks listed a very high implied move around 28.48%, indicating large expected volatility and high reversal risk.
ranked_companies.analyst_revision_summary: Coverage is limited; investors are likely to focus more on filing quality, ARR/RPO, and FY2027 guidance than consensus EPS.

qualified_names.why: Consensus absolute score above 30, reversal risk below 40, low disparity, and strongest evidence quality.

excluded_high_reversal_or_weak_names.reason: Score qualifies but reversal risk is above 40 because the beat was preannounced and filing/balance-sheet risk is high.
Score qualifies but reversal risk is above 40 due to cannabis volatility, low price, and thin margin for error.
Score qualifies on downside but reversal risk is above 40 and relief upside is plausible if the filing is clean.
Score below 30 and reversal risk above 40.
Neutral score and reversal risk above 40.
Neutral score, low confidence, and reversal risk above 40.

research_allocation_view.plain_statement: This is a research prioritization model, not financial advice.

consensus_method_note: Disparity is a 0-100 disagreement measure across three independent research passes using direction-score and reversal-risk spread. WLY had low disparity and qualified for the research basket. QMCO, HITI, DOMO, PLAY, UXIN, and VNCE had moderate or higher effective caution because reversal risk, filing risk, liquidity, or mixed evidence reduced allocation priority.
disclaimer_text: This is research, not financial advice. Earnings reactions are highly uncertain and can be driven by market positioning, guidance, macro conditions, and management commentary rather than reported results alone.
